statement.en: Geometric Derivation of the Gravitational Constant from Lambda under Radiative Boundary Normalization
statement.summary: In this work, we present a geometric boundary framework that explicitly derives the gravitational relation (G) from the cosmological constant (Λ) through a scale-independent surface normalization. Starting from radiative decoupling surfaces, we identify a dimensionless boundary relation converging to the exact mass-independent identity π³/15. Extending this boundary condition to the cosmic horizon yields the master constraint ΛR_H² = π³/15. By incorporating holographic tiling of the horizon area, we extract an explicit algebraic expression for G, governed entirely by the discrete information bound (N) of the macroscopic surface. Within this formulation, the canonical ~ 10^120 vacuum-energy discrepancy is mathematically resolved not as an energy divergence, but as the exact dimensional scal

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The paper presents a novel geometric framework with specific numerical claims (π³/15 convergence, ΛR_H² master constraint, G derivation from Λ) that are internally coherent but lack independent peer validation, published cross-category (cs.AI filed but physics content), with incomplete abstract. The approach is mathematically structured but extraordinary claims (resolving 10^120 discrepancy) require external confirmation. Status: preprint-stage provisional.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 重力は宇宙の境界に刻まれた離散情報の幾何学的な影である。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 地平線から中心へ向かう光の糸が、量子情報と古典重力を結ぶ。
